check: A named technical owner and business owner are accountable for the service
recommended_action: Assign owners before changing production behavior

check: The service boundary and supported business capabilities are documented
recommended_action: Document callers, dependencies, and business responsibilities

check: Consumers, traffic patterns, and critical journeys are inventoried
evidence_required: Consumer inventory with request volume and criticality
recommended_action: Capture synchronous, asynchronous, batch, and manual consumers

check: Modernization goals have measurable success criteria
recommended_action: Define targets for reliability, delivery speed, cost, and maintainability

check: The Java runtime and Spring Boot line are vendor-supported
recommended_action: Choose a supported target and document the upgrade sequence

check: Dependencies are inventoried with known vulnerabilities and end-of-life status
recommended_action: Remove unused libraries and plan high-risk upgrades separately

check: Application startup and shutdown are deterministic
recommended_action: Fail fast on invalid configuration and drain traffic before shutdown

check: JVM and container resource settings are explicit and reproducible
recommended_action: Version configuration and test it under representative load

check: Transactional boundaries and data ownership are explicit
recommended_action: Resolve cross-service writes before extracting components

check: Module boundaries prevent business logic from depending directly on delivery frameworks
recommended_action: Introduce enforceable package or module boundaries

check: External integrations are isolated behind stable interfaces
recommended_action: Wrap remote clients, messaging, filesystem, and vendor SDKs

check: Shared libraries have a clear owner, compatibility policy, and removal path
recommended_action: Prefer small stable contracts over shared business logic

check: Database changes are backward-compatible across the rollout window
recommended_action: Separate additive and destructive schema changes

check: Slow and high-volume queries have measured plans and ownership
recommended_action: Optimize using representative parameters and validate result equality

check: Connection pools, transaction timeouts, and retry behavior are bounded
recommended_action: Set limits from measured concurrency and database capacity

check: Retention, archival, and cleanup jobs are documented and monitored
recommended_action: Bound table growth and rehearse cleanup failure recovery

check: Retries are bounded, classified, and safe for the operation
recommended_action: Retry only transient failures with backoff and jitter

check: Remote calls have timeouts and failure isolation
recommended_action: Define connect, read, and total deadlines plus circuit or bulkhead policy

check: Asynchronous consumers handle duplicates, poison messages, and replay
recommended_action: Make handlers idempotent and define replay ownership

check: Capacity limits and degradation behavior are known
recommended_action: Test saturation and preserve critical journeys first

check: Secrets are externalized, rotated, and absent from source and images
recommended_action: Use a managed secret store and short-lived credentials where possible

check: Authentication and authorization are enforced at every exposed boundary
recommended_action: Centralize policy while retaining endpoint-level enforcement

check: Input validation, output encoding, and sensitive-data handling are explicit
recommended_action: Reject invalid input and prevent sensitive fields from logs and responses

check: Software artifacts have provenance and automated vulnerability checks
evidence_required: SBOM, image scan, and signed build evidence
recommended_action: Gate releases on actionable severity thresholds

check: Critical business behavior has automated regression coverage
recommended_action: Protect behavior before refactoring implementation

check: Database and integration behavior is tested against realistic infrastructure
recommended_action: Use disposable infrastructure and production-compatible schemas

check: Consumer and provider contracts are versioned and verified
recommended_action: Validate compatibility before independent deployment

check: Performance tests have representative data and pass/fail thresholds
recommended_action: Track latency, throughput, saturation, and error rate

check: Service-level indicators and objectives exist for critical journeys
recommended_action: Measure user-visible success, latency, and availability

check: Logs are structured, correlated, and exclude sensitive data
recommended_action: Propagate correlation context across HTTP, messaging, and jobs

check: Metrics expose traffic, errors, latency, saturation, and dependency health
recommended_action: Alert on symptoms and capacity risk, not raw log volume

check: Traces connect expensive or failure-prone distributed paths
recommended_action: Instrument boundaries and retain enough context for diagnosis

check: The deployment process is automated, repeatable, and auditable
evidence_required: Pipeline run and immutable artifact reference
recommended_action: Build once, promote the same artifact, and record provenance

check: Rollout and rollback procedures are tested
recommended_action: Use incremental rollout with explicit abort thresholds

check: Configuration compatibility is validated before deployment
recommended_action: Version configuration and reject missing or invalid values

check: Database, application, and message changes have an ordered release plan
recommended_action: Keep old and new versions interoperable during rollout

check: On-call ownership and incident escalation are defined
recommended_action: Assign responders before production migration

check: Runbooks cover top failure modes and dependency outages
evidence_required: Tested runbooks with commands and expected signals
recommended_action: Write decision-oriented recovery steps and rehearse them

check: Backup restoration and disaster recovery are tested
recommended_action: Measure actual RPO and RTO instead of relying on configuration

check: Operational cost and capacity trends are reviewed
recommended_action: Track unit cost and define scaling or optimization triggers
